artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found UGR's CEPRUD AI recommendations page, related CEPRUD guidance pages, and the BOUGR publication of the recommendations. Claims are scoped as recommendations/guidance because the UGR source text says the document is not normative. Course, research/journal, and external catalog pages were rejected for claim extraction.

artifacts.claimText: UGR has published Council of Government-presented recommendations for AI use, but the document says it is not normative and does not establish what is permitted or prohibited.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: La Universidad de Granada proporciona a PDI y PTGAS la oportunidad de utilizar la version protegida del chat Copilot accediendo con la cuenta institucional de Microsoft (@ms.ugr.es). La principal ventaja de esta IA Generativa es que las preguntas y datos que utilicemos para interactuar con el modelo no seran reutilizados posteriormente para reentrenar el modelo.

artifacts.title: University of Granada AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Official UGR sources support a staged record for AI-use recommendations covering non-normative status, student disclosure and integrity guidance, privacy and institutional-tool preferences, protected Copilot access for PDI/PTGAS, research transparency/supervision, and CEPRUD tool-evaluation guidance. The crawl did not find a central binding university AI policy that establishes mandatory permissions or prohibitions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
UGR's recommendation document says it is not normative; staged claims therefore use recommendation language and do not assert a binding central AI policy.
Course offerings, journal/research pages, and an external Notion catalog were rejected or not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
